source_summary: ATM cash withdrawal is available after authentication, capped at $500 per day, and must reject insufficient-funds requests.
assumptions: The ATM can determine the customer's remaining daily withdrawal allowance before dispensing cash.
out_of_scope: Cash deposits and balance inquiry scenarios

covered_behaviors: Successful withdrawal within remaining daily limit
gaps: Card retention behavior after repeated invalid PIN attempts is not specified.

acceptance_criteria.title: Dispense cash within the remaining daily limit
acceptance_criteria.given: A customer is authenticated and has at least $100 available balance and daily withdrawal allowance remaining
acceptance_criteria.then: The ATM dispenses $100, records the withdrawal, and decreases the remaining daily allowance by $100

acceptance_criteria.title: Reject requests that would exceed the daily limit
acceptance_criteria.given: A customer is authenticated and has already withdrawn $450 today
acceptance_criteria.then: The ATM declines the request and explains that the $500 daily limit would be exceeded

acceptance_criteria.title: Reject withdrawals when the account balance is insufficient
acceptance_criteria.given: A customer is authenticated and the requested amount is greater than the available account balance
acceptance_criteria.then: The ATM declines the withdrawal and leaves the account balance unchanged
